situationalOpenings.name: Friendly greeting + reason for writing
phrases: How have you been? I hope you and your parents are in good health.
How are you? I hope you've been doing well since our last meeting.
I am writing this email to inform you of an incident which took place on...
I am writing you this email to invite you to...
I am writing to request permission to make an announcement about...

situationalOpenings.name: Persuasive hook
phrases: This competition will be a piece of cake for you since you love...
I am sure you will do well in this competition if you take part.
I would love it if you could take part in this event with me.
It will be extremely meaningful if we can do our part in contributing to...
Now is the perfect chance to increase the number and variety of...

situationalOpenings.name: Polite request to authority
phrases: I would like to ask you to consider my classmate for the award.
I am writing on behalf of my class to seek your permission to...
I would really appreciate it if you would keep the plan under wraps.
I would also like to ask for your help in rehearsing the...
May I respectfully request your approval for this project?

situationalConnectors.name: Adding a reason
phrases: As participants are required to...
Hence, I am asking you to accompany me instead.
Furthermore, since you like action movies, I am sure you will enjoy it.
As we hope to give the teachers a pleasant surprise on that day...
It is important that we inform our fellow pupils about the event as...

situationalConnectors.name: Giving examples / Details
phrases: Besides that, I saw him performing another good deed when...
First, they have to scan the QR code provided on the post...
Second, they have to check for the expiry date of their food item.
For every comment uploaded, the bookstore will donate a new book...
In the form of a food donation drive, the project welcomes pupils to...

situationalConnectors.name: Acknowledging objections / Conditions
phrases: However, John refused and was unhappy as he was first in the queue.
Even if it seems difficult at the beginning, I can learn something new.
Although they had been to the beach several times before...
If you want to participate in the competition, you need to register by...
Before donating a food item at the booth, it is important to note...

situationalClosings.name: Polite call to action
phrases: I hope you will consider him for the award.
Do let me know if you will be able to join me as soon as possible.
Do let us know before then if you would be keen to take part...
I hope to hear good news from you before then!
I look forward to hearing good news from you soon!

situationalClosings.name: Warm sign-off
phrases: Thank you for your kind attention.
We hope to receive your favourable response.
I would really appreciate your help in this matter and I look forward to your favourable reply!
Hope to hear from you soon!
Yours sincerely,

continuousOpenings.name: Weather / atmosphere
phrases: The clear blue sky was a welcome sight which lifted their mood.
The scenic view of the sea unfolded before their eyes.
The cooling sea breeze blew in his face.
The weather was perfect when they stepped out of the car.
A thick blanket of clouds hung over the city, mirroring my gloomy mood.

continuousOpenings.name: In-the-moment action
phrases: Clara was on her way home from school when she heard someone calling her name.
I got home after school one day and was greeted by my brother at the door.
I eagerly opened the envelope, wondering what it could contain.
The girls sprung up from the sofa and squealed with delight.
I could hardly stay still in my seat the whole day.

continuousOpenings.name: Dialogue opener
phrases: "What's the matter, Lisa?" Clara asked, wondering if her classmate was all right.
"What's this?" I asked, waving an envelope I had found on the dining table.
"Roger, go to the nearby shopping centre to help me get some groceries," Mother instructed.
"Have you set up an appointment yet?" I prodded my mother.
"Look at what we have bought for you!" their father exclaimed.

continuousOpenings.name: Reflective flashback
phrases: Being thankful does not always come from big surprises or expensive gifts.
Sometimes, simple moments in our daily lives remind us to appreciate what we have.
I experienced such a moment on the MRT train last month.
It had a special place in my heart for it was the first thing I had bought with my own money.
It all started when Tommy's teacher assigned a project that required researching local history.

showDontTell.name: Fear / nervousness
phrases: Her face was pale.
He resisted the urge to panic.
I stood at the starting line, a bundle of nerves.
My heart leapt into my throat.
I froze mid-step, unable to process what I had just seen.

showDontTell.name: Anger / frustration
phrases: I wanted to lash out at my brother for his carelessness.
My anger subsided only after seeing his pitiful expression.
He flatly denied the accusation and said that he was lying.
I threw in the towel and returned to my room.
My fists clenched and my jaw tightened.

showDontTell.name: Sadness / regret
phrases: His voice trailed off guiltily.
He had a pitiful expression on his face.
A heavy lump formed in my throat.
Tears welled up uncontrollably in my eyes.
I looked at him with beseeching eyes.

showDontTell.name: Joy / excitement
phrases: She gave a whoop of joy.
They squealed with delight when they saw the paper bag.
My head was still reeling from the exciting announcement.
I tailed my mother out of the house, a skip in my step.
My face broke into the widest, most radiant grin.

showDontTell.name: Surprise / shock
phrases: I could not believe my eyes when I saw the new figurine staring at me.
I was momentarily stunned, not knowing how to react.
My jaw dropped in sheer disbelief.
She looked around in surprise, not used to the attention.
I turned around and froze.

showDontTell.name: Embarrassment / shame
phrases: She smiled back sheepishly and patted her rumbling stomach.
He had a guilty expression on his face.
My cheeks burned crimson.
I wished the ground would swallow me whole.
I apologised profusely for the mess.

showDontTell.name: Relief
phrases: He sighed with relief.
He could feel his muscles relax as his fear and anxiety evaporated.
A wave of relief washed over me.
Her voice was gentle but filled with relief.
I exhaled the breath I had been holding.

sensoryDescriptions.name: Sight
phrases: a grease-stained paper bag
gaily decorated with colourful balloons and streamers
the scenic view of the sea unfolded before their eyes
the clear blue sky was a welcome sight
the glistening sea reflecting the orange sunset

sensoryDescriptions.name: Sound
phrases: high-pitched barking, followed by the frantic running of a little black puppy
greeted by raucous laughter and loud chatter
incessant pen-clicking, foot tapping and intermittent sighs
the deafening cheers of the crowd
the familiar sound of the door being unlocked

sensoryDescriptions.name: Smell / taste
phrases: the aroma of food wafted in the air
savour the juicy patties sandwiched between the soft sesame seed buns
the special house sauce oozed out from the burger
she smelt of baby powder and was a delight to carry
an array of mouthwatering dishes laden on the table

sensoryDescriptions.name: Touch / texture
phrases: the cooling sea breeze blew in his face
it gave the sandwich a nice crunchy texture
the soft sesame seed buns
clammy palms and a hammering heart
the rough bark scraped against my palm

dialogueTags: looked at her classmate with beseeching eyes

continuousClosings.name: Reflection / lesson learned
phrases: That was something no amount of money in the world could buy.
He learnt valuable life lessons about responsibility, discipline and the joy of giving back.
That simple train ride taught me that kindness does not need to be grand.
Trying something new was not bad at all!
I realised that I, too, was thankful for the small actions that brighten someone's day.

continuousClosings.name: Resolution / change
phrases: Tommy had changed for the better, and it all began with that simple history project.
His journey from a lazy, disorganised video gamer to an active, motivated young boy was an inspiration.
I now know that if I keep trying and practising, I can learn something new.
Basketball is no longer just my sister's sport. It is mine too.
I knew the puppy would be in good hands.

continuousClosings.name: Vivid final image
phrases: I gave him a warm embrace, thanking him for the gift.
As the car drove off, Justin leaned against the window, admiring the view of the orange sunset.
The mishap which happened to my dress only served to make it more memorable!
Hand in hand, the boys went back to the supermarket to continue their shopping.
It was finally the moment I had been waiting for.

sentenceVariety.weak: I was very happy when I won the prize.
sentenceVariety.strong: As we proceeded up on stage to collect the prize and the trophy, I looked at my team members in appreciation, proud of the teamwork we had displayed.
sentenceVariety.highlight: As we proceeded up on stage

sentenceVariety.strong: I closed my eyes to savour the juicy patties sandwiched between the soft sesame seed buns.
sentenceVariety.technique: Use sensory details and specific nouns/adjectives

sentenceVariety.weak: I was very excited to see the rabbit.
sentenceVariety.strong: I could hardly stay still in my seat the whole day, my mind filled with thoughts about the rabbit.
sentenceVariety.technique: Show excitement through restless physical actions
sentenceVariety.highlight: hardly stay still in my seat

sentenceVariety.weak: He was lazy and played games all day.
sentenceVariety.strong: He had always been known for his love of video games, and it seemed like he spent every waking moment glued to his mobile phone.
As the car turned the corner, the scenic view of the sea unfolded before their eyes, accompanied by the cooling sea breeze.
sentenceVariety.technique: Reveal the setting dynamically through character movement
sentenceVariety.highlight: scenic view of the sea unfolded

sentenceVariety.weak: I was very scared when I lost my wallet.
sentenceVariety.strong: To my horror, I was unable to find it, and I had to resist the urge to panic.
sentenceVariety.technique: Name the emotion directly as an introductory phrase

sentenceVariety.strong: Suddenly, my cousin coughed and before I knew it, she had thrown up milk all over me!
sentenceVariety.technique: Build anticipation with a transition and sudden action

sentenceVariety.weak: I waited a long time for school to end.
sentenceVariety.strong: Seconds stretched into minutes and minutes stretched into hours as my eyes remained fixated on the clock.
sentenceVariety.technique: Use repetition and time distortion to show impatience

sentenceVariety.weak: I ran out of the school very fast.
sentenceVariety.strong: Once the school bell rang, signalling the end of classes, I shot like a bullet out of the school building.
sentenceVariety.technique: Use a simile and a clear trigger event

sentenceVariety.weak: I searched the store for the things I needed.
sentenceVariety.strong: Quickly scouring the aisles to locate my targets, I speedily scooped up the goods.
sentenceVariety.technique: Use strong action verbs and a participial phrase
